みず.text: 水 — one of the first kanji learned, a picture of flowing water.

はな.text: 花 (flower) and 鼻 (nose) are both 'hana' — same sound, different kanji.

びょういん.text: びょういん (hospital) vs びよういん (salon) — the small ょ decides.

がっこう.text: Small っ plus long おう — two spelling traps in one word: school.

せんせい.text: えい is written with い but sounds like long e — a teacher.

ちず.text: Written ず, not づ — the rendaku spelling rule; a map.

でんしゃ.text: 電車 — electric + car; a train.

とけい.text: えい, a long-e written with い — a clock.

つくえ.text: 机 — a desk; note there is no small っ here.
